approved_account_debtors: FMC Network UAE Management Consultancy LLC
detail: The Klaim tape has no column identifying the insurance company (Account Debtor / payer) for each deal. The 'Group' column contains healthcare provider names (143 unique sellers), NOT insurance company names. Product column only has 'claim' and 'invoice'. Without a payer column, the platform CANNOT: (1) verify that all receivables are owed by approved Account Debtors, (2) monitor the 10% non-eligible debtor concentration limit per MRPA Concentration Limits (d), (3) flag receivables from unapproved insurance companies as ineligible.

expected_payment_date_gap.detail: April 15 tape (8,080 deals, full portfolio) adds 'Expected collection days' (28-364d range, mean 94d, median 87d, 100% coverage) and 'Collection days so far' (-448 to 1084d range). Enables: (1) direct DPD for PAR — method reports 'direct' not 'proxy', (2) temporal Paid vs Due covenant — filters to deals with expected payment date in period, (3) DSO Operational = true_dso - expected_collection_days per deal (method 'direct').
expected_payment_date_gap.remaining_gap: Older tapes (pre-April 2026) still use proxy DPD. Platform gracefully degrades to proxy when column absent.

recommendations: STILL OPEN: Request Klaim add an 'Insurance Company' or 'Payer' column — Account Debtor gap remains
RESOLVED: Expected collection days now in April 15 tape — direct DPD for PAR working
RESOLVED: April 15 tape confirmed as full portfolio (8,080 deals, up from 7,697 in March)
The 10% non-eligible debtor concentration limit STILL cannot be enforced from tape data alone
Consider adding payer identification to the Integration API invoice schema (extra_data JSONB field)

mrpa_concentration_limit_d.rule: Maximum 10% of Pool Balance may be owed by Account Debtors NOT on the approved list
